2606.24669.title: LaGO: Latent Action Guidance for Online Reinforcement Learning
2606.24669.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have shown strong potential for planning and sequential decision-making, but prior work often relies on using them as direct controllers, which requires precise action generation and can be unreliable in practice. This paper proposes Latent Action Guidance for Online Reinforcement Learning (LaGO), a framework that uses a pretrained LLM as a latent action prior to softly guide online policy optimization, rather than treating the LLM as an explicit planner or controller. Experiments on both a discrete-control benchmark, CLEVR-Robot, and a continuous-control benchmark, Meta-World, demonstrate that LaGO consistently improves both reward and success rate over Vanilla PPO. In particular, LaGO increases the average success rate from 15.1% to 27.2% on CLEVR-Robot and from 2.7% to 15.2% on Meta-World. Our analysis further shows that stronger pretrained LLMs provide more effective guidance, suggesting that LLM knowledge can improve planning and online decision-making.
2606.24669.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）顯示出在規劃和序列決策方面的強大潛力，但先前的研究通常依賴將它們作為直接控制器，這需要精確的行動生成，並且在實踐中可能不可靠。本文提出了在線強化學習的潛在行動引導（LaGO），這是一個使用預訓練LLM作為潛在行動先驗來柔性引導在線策略優化的框架，而不是將LLM視為明確的規劃者或控制器。在離散控制基準CLEVR-Robot和連續控制基準Meta-World上的實驗表明，LaGO在獎勵和成功率上始終優於Vanilla PPO。特別是，LaGO將CLEVR-Robot的平均成功率從15.1%提高到27.2%，將Meta-World的平均成功率從2.7%提高到15.2%。我們的分析進一步顯示，較強的預訓練LLM提供了更有效的引導，這表明LLM知識可以改善規劃和在線決策。
2606.24669.author: Kuan-Yen Liu et.al.
2606.24669.authors: Kuan-Yen Liu, Ren-Jyun Huang, Ti-Rong Wu
